user_query:
Please review the households in input/households.json and their preferences in input/preferences.yaml. Using State Farm insurance offerings, state-by-state availability nuances, and discount programs, create a tailored set of recommendations and next steps for each household. Do not provide prices or attempt to process any transactions.

Write the following deliverables under output/ exactly as specified:

1) output/coverage_recommendations.csv
- Columns (exact order): customer_id,state,product_line,recommended_coverages,key_discounts,notes
- Include one row per household and cover the relevant product lines (e.g., auto, homeowners/renters/condo). If a new homeowners policy is requested in California, Massachusetts, or Rhode Island, still list product_line as "homeowners" but include the phrases "not available" and the state name in notes, with an alternative suggestion (e.g., speak with an agent about options).
- Add auto discounts appropriate to each situation in key_discounts (e.g., Drive Safe & Save for telematics, Steer Clear for young drivers under 25 with clean records, Good Student where applicable). If net_worth is at least 1000000, add an umbrella liability recommendation in recommended_coverages.

2) output/proposal.md
- Organize with clear H1-level sections per household using the format: "## <customer_id> - <state>".
- In each section, briefly explain recommended coverages using State Farm terminology (e.g., Liability, Collision, Comprehensive for auto; Dwelling, Personal Property, Liability for home; and Term Life/Whole Life/Universal Life as relevant). Explain trade-offs and why each applies.
- Call out availability caveats by state when relevant (e.g., new homeowners policy availability in CA/MA/RI). If life insurance is discussed for NY/MA/WI, note the insurer entity point (that these states are served by State Farm Life and Accident Assurance Company).
- Provide instructions to obtain quotes via online, the mobile app, and through a local agent, and include a link text to "statefarm.com/agent".
- Include a "Claims" subsection listing at least two filing channels (e.g., online at statefarm.com/claims and the mobile app).
- Include an "Important" subsection with this exact sentence: "This is informational guidance only; for quotes, purchases, or claims, contact State Farm directly via statefarm.com, the mobile app, or a licensed agent.".

3) output/next_steps_checklist.txt
- A concise 5–10 item checklist that includes:
  - A line starting with "Agent appointment:" and the text "statefarm.com/agent".
  - Separate steps to gather information for auto and home quotes.
  - A step to enroll in Drive Safe & Save if applicable.

4) output/assumptions.json
- A JSON object with keys: version (string), customers_count (number), disclaimer_acknowledged (boolean true), notes (string).
- Set customers_count to the number of households you addressed and ensure it matches the number of rows in output/coverage_recommendations.csv and the number of household sections (## headings) in output/proposal.md.

Make sure your recommendations reflect the data in the input files, stay strictly within informational guidance (no quotes, no prices), and avoid collecting personal information. Be explicit about state-specific availability constraints and which discounts apply.
